seoTitle: How to Set Up Zendesk Automations for Stale Tickets
metaDescription: Learn how to set up Zendesk automations that run hourly, using loop-breakers and preview matching to safely follow up on stale or pending tickets.
intro: Tickets that sit without a follow-up can quietly damage customer satisfaction. Zendesk's time-based automation engine checks your queues on a schedule and applies actions automatically, without needing an agent to notice the delay. This guide covers building a safe, loop-proof automation.

steps.heading: Open the Automations panel
steps.body: In Admin Center, expand Objects and Rules and choose the Automations sidebar panel.

steps.heading: Create a new automation
steps.body: Click Add Automation and enter a clear, descriptive administrative title.

steps.heading: Set your base condition
steps.body: Under Meet All conditions, map target parameters like Status Is Pending.

steps.heading: Add a time-elapsed trigger
steps.body: Add a condition such as Hours Since Pending Is Greater Than 48 to catch stale tickets.

steps.heading: Add a loop-breaker
steps.body: Add a guard condition, such as Tags Do Not Contain a tracking label, so the rule doesn't fire repeatedly on the same ticket.

steps.heading: Set actions and preview before saving
steps.body: Configure the action to alert an agent and add the tracking tag, click Preview Match to check affected volume, then click Create Automation.

faq.question: Why do I need a loop-breaker condition?
faq.answer: Without one, the automation can keep re-applying its action to the same ticket every time it runs, creating a messy repeated loop.

faq.question: How often does a time-based automation check tickets?
faq.answer: Zendesk's automation engine scans pending queues on an hourly basis.

faq.question: Should I use 'Is' or 'Greater than' for time conditions?
faq.answer: Greater than operators are generally safer, since exact 'Is' windows can be skipped during busy system intervals.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many tickets an automation can update?
faq.answer: Yes, Zendesk enforces processing ceilings, so it's worth understanding the hourly ticket and update caps for your plan.
